summary: User asks how to replace black-colored pixels in an SKSurface with transparent/empty before compositing via DrawSurface, wanting to know if SKBlendMode or SKColorFilter is the right tool.

analysis.summary: The reporter wants to 'erase' previously drawn black circles on an off-screen GPU-backed SKSurface before compositing it onto the main canvas. The correct approach is to use SKBlendMode.Clear on the erasing paint, or alternatively draw the black circles last with SKBlendMode.DstOut to cut them out. SKCanvas.Clear() is also available to reset the whole surface.
analysis.rationale: Issue title is tagged [QUESTION] and the body asks 'how can I do that?' with no description of broken behavior. The SKBlendMode and SKColorFilter APIs exist and work as expected. This is a usage question.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKCanvas.Clear() and Clear(SKColor) exist and reset the entire canvas to transparent/a given color — confirmed API available.
SKCanvas.DrawColor(SKColor, SKBlendMode) exists — allows drawing a color with any blend mode including Clear over a region.
SKPaint.BlendMode property exists — setting BlendMode = SKBlendMode.Clear on a paint will erase pixels at the drawn shape's location, making them transparent.

keySignals.text: how can i do that? is it using SKBlendMode or ColorFilter to achieve this
keySignals.interpretation: Usage question — reporter is asking which API to use, not reporting broken behavior.

keySignals.text: replace the black colored dots (can be several) with Empty or Transparent
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter wants selective per-shape erasure using blend modes.

workarounds: Use a paint with BlendMode = SKBlendMode.Clear when drawing the 'eraser' circles — this punches transparent holes at exactly those positions.
Use canvas.Clear() to reset the whole surface to transparent, then redraw without the black circles.
Draw the same circles with a transparent color and BlendMode = SKBlendMode.Src to overwrite.

resolution.hypothesis: Reporter needs to use SKBlendMode.Clear on the SKPaint when drawing the circles they want to erase. The surface must use premultiplied alpha (default for GPU surfaces) for Clear blend mode to work correctly.

proposals.title: Use SKBlendMode.Clear paint to erase circles
proposals.description: Create a separate paint with BlendMode set to SKBlendMode.Clear and draw the same circles at the same positions to punch out transparent holes.

proposals.title: Reset entire surface and redraw without black
proposals.description: Call canvas.Clear() on the surface canvas before redrawing to reset all pixels to transparent, then skip the black circles.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use SKBlendMode.Clear paint to erase circles
resolution.recommendedReason: Most precise — erases only the desired positions without redrawing everything.

actionability.reason: This is a usage question with a clear answer using existing SKBlendMode.Clear API. No bug is present.

actions.description: Apply question type and SkiaSharp area labels
Answer the question with SKBlendMode.Clear pattern
